canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, prompt engineering is predominantly characterized as the observable behavior of either generating new textual inputs or modifying existing ones to guide language models toward desired outcomes. The most common form is the generation of new content, which encompasses a wide range of actions from producing search queries for retrieval and formulating questions to resolve ambiguity, to creating novel mathematical problems or task instructions. For instance, several papers define this as asking clarifying questions to "elicit more information" ("Modeling Future Conversation Turns to Teach LLMs to Ask Clarifying Questions"), while others focus on generating new data for model training. A second, widely discussed aspect involves the modification and optimization of existing prompts. This includes rewriting queries to be more "search-engine-friendly" ("MMSearch: Unveiling the Potential of Large Models as Multi-modal Search Engines"), rephrasing prompts to bypass safety measures, or systematically refining them to improve task performance. The literature describes several distinct optimization methods, such as "soft prompt tuning," which optimizes trainable embedding vectors, and "black-box prompt tuning," which adjusts prompts based only on model outputs. The effectiveness of these behaviors is operationalized and evaluated using benchmarks including MS-COCO, MovieLens, and SQuAD v1.1. Prompt engineering is frequently studied alongside disambiguation and mathematical reasoning, and some work reports that specific techniques can influence model generalization, interpretability, and flexibility.
expert_removed_reason: method/engineering — not a construct/measurement/behavior of LLM ability

definitions.text: The observable act of generating relevant and specific questions to explore a dataset in pursuit of a high-level goal.
definitions.paper: InsightBench: Evaluating Business Analytics Agents Through Multi-Step Insight Generation

definitions.text: Producing textual prompts that are fed to a task-specific language model to elicit desired outputs.
definitions.paper: Pareto Prompt Optimization

definitions.text: The observable act of generating questions to acquire additional information needed to improve confidence in a decision.
definitions.paper: BIRD: A Trustworthy Bayesian Inference Framework for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of creating new mathematical problems, often based on seed examples from an existing dataset.
definitions.paper: OpenMathInstruct-2: Accelerating AI for Math with Massive Open-Source Instruction Data

definitions.text: The task of producing a natural language instruction that accurately describes a given trajectory through an environment.
definitions.paper: Bootstrapping Language-Guided Navigation Learning with Self-Refining Data Flywheel

definitions.text: The observable action of a model producing a textual query to be sent to a retriever or search engine.
definitions.paper: Grounding by Trying: LLMs with Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced Retrieval

definitions.text: Generating a textual prompt that, when provided to a text-to-image model, reproduces a given target image.
definitions.paper: Visually Guided Decoding: Gradient-Free Hard Prompt Inversion with Language Models

definitions.text: The observable action of producing a novel task description in natural language, intended to be both learnable for an agent and distinct from previously seen tasks.
definitions.paper: OMNI-EPIC: Open-endedness via Models of human Notions of Interestingness with Environments Programmed in Code

definitions.text: The act of generating a question in response to a user's query to resolve ambiguity and elicit more information.
definitions.paper: Modeling Future Conversation Turns to Teach LLMs to Ask Clarifying Questions

definitions.text: Rewriting a user query into a clearer or search-engine-friendly form, sometimes incorporating information extracted from an image.
definitions.paper: MMSearch: Unveiling the Potential of Large Models as Multi-modal Search Engines

definitions.text: The task of modifying an existing language expression to correct inaccuracies and better align it with a target visual object.
definitions.paper: Re-Aligning Language to Visual Objects with an Agentic Workflow

definitions.text: Rephrasing an input prompt while preserving its meaning, often to make malicious intent less detectable.
definitions.paper: One Model Transfer to All: On Robust Jailbreak Prompts Generation against LLMs

definitions.text: The observable behavior of rewriting a text prompt to make it easier for a model to interpret, while preserving the original meaning.
definitions.paper: Rare-to-Frequent: Unlocking Compositional Generation Power of Diffusion Models on Rare Concepts with LLM Guidance

definitions.text: The action of restating a given problem or question, often to clarify conditions or focus on key information.
definitions.paper: Mutual Reasoning Makes Smaller LLMs Stronger Problem-Solver

definitions.text: The observable action of reformulating an input query to improve its clarity or comprehensibility before attempting to solve it.
definitions.paper: DOTS: Learning to Reason Dynamically in LLMs via Optimal Reasoning Trajectories Search

definitions.text: The observable action of adjusting input tokens in a query, such as removing noisy or correcting erroneous tokens.
definitions.paper: Unified Parameter-Efficient Unlearning for LLMs

definitions.text: Shortening a long, descriptive text prompt into a more concise version while preserving its core semantic meaning for image generation.
definitions.paper: Visually Guided Decoding: Gradient-Free Hard Prompt Inversion with Language Models

definitions.text: Refining a prompt to improve a language model's performance on a target task.
definitions.paper: GReaTer: Gradients Over Reasoning Makes Smaller Language Models Strong Prompt Optimizers

definitions.text: The task of creating effective search queries to send to a retriever to obtain relevant information for a given question.
definitions.paper: C-3PO: Compact Plug-and-Play Proxy Optimization to Achieve Human-like Retrieval-Augmented Generation

definitions.text: The process of adjusting sequences of discrete tokens (prompts) through interaction with a black-box LLM to improve task performance without access to model gradients.
definitions.paper: FedOne: Query-Efficient Federated Learning for Black-box Discrete Prompt Learning

definitions.text: Adjusting input prompts to a large language model based solely on its outputs, without access to internal model states or gradients, typically via API queries.
definitions.paper: FedOne: Query-Efficient Federated Learning for Black-box Discrete Prompt Learning

definitions.text: The process of optimizing trainable embedding vectors prepended to the input of a frozen language model to adapt it to a downstream task.
definitions.paper: Efficient and Privacy-Preserving Soft Prompt Transfer for LLMs

definitions.text: The process of combining structural knowledge from a document-level knowledge graph and semantic context from retrieved sentences into a unified prompt for LLM input.
definitions.paper: DocKS-RAG: Optimizing Document-Level Relation Extraction through LLM-Enhanced Hybrid Prompt Tuning

definitions.text: Modifying existing prompts through rewriting or transformation to increase their complexity or learning value for the model.
definitions.paper: Reward-Guided Prompt Evolving in Reinforcement Learning for LLMs

definitions.text: Producing alternative visual prompts from misleading regions that are used to contrast against the original input prompt during decoding.
definitions.paper: CPCF: A Cross-Prompt Contrastive Framework for Referring Multimodal Large Language Models

definitions.text: Producing a clarifying question instead of directly answering when the user request is ambiguous or underspecified.
definitions.paper: Learning to Clarify: Multi-turn Conversations with Action-Based Contrastive Self-Training

definitions.text: Producing questions intended to elicit missing task specifications that distinguish among multiple compatible solutions.
definitions.paper: Active Task Disambiguation with LLMs

definitions.text: Generating follow-up inquiries to gather additional patient information when initial context is insufficient.
definitions.paper: MediQ: Question-Asking LLMs and a Benchmark for Reliable Interactive Clinical Reasoning

definitions.text: The behavior of asking questions that encourage detailed, narrative responses rather than simple yes/no answers.
definitions.paper: Position: Medical Large Language Model Benchmarks Should Prioritize Construct Validity

definitions.text: Generating prompts that encourage step-by-step reasoning before answering a problem.
definitions.paper: Localized Zeroth-Order Prompt Optimization

definitions.text: Generating natural-sounding user queries from relational and textual requirements for benchmark construction.
definitions.paper: STaRK: Benchmarking LLM Retrieval on Textual and Relational Knowledge Bases

definitions.text: Posing free-form questions that elicit descriptive answers, rather than binary yes/no responses, to gather richer information about a hidden object.
definitions.paper: ChameleonLLMs: User Personas Influence Chatbot Personality Shifts

definitions.text: The process by which an LLM generates synthetic queries from unlabeled passages to form augmented training pairs for retrieval models.
definitions.paper: Extracting and Combining Abilities For Building Multi-lingual Ability-enhanced Large Language Models

emnlp25: Reading Between the Prompts: How Stereotypes ShapeLLM’s Implicit Personalization

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, prompt engineering is predominantly characterized as the observable behavior of either generating new textual inputs or modifying existing ones to guide language models toward desired outcomes. The most common form is the generation of new content, which encompasses a wide range of actions from producing search queries for retrieval and formulating questions to resolve ambiguity, to creating novel mathematical problems or task instructions. For instance, several papers define this as asking clarifying questions to "elicit more information" ("Modeling Future Conversation Turns to Teach LLMs to Ask Clarifying Questions"), while others focus on generating new data for model training. A second, widely discussed aspect involves the modification and optimization of existing prompts. This includes rewriting queries to be more "search-engine-friendly" ("MMSearch: Unveiling the Potential of Large Models as Multi-modal Search Engines"), rephrasing prompts to bypass safety measures, or systematically refining them to improve task performance. The literature describes several distinct optimization methods, such as "soft prompt tuning," which optimizes trainable embedding vectors, and "black-box prompt tuning," which adjusts prompts based only on model outputs. The effectiveness of these behaviors is operationalized and evaluated using benchmarks including MS-COCO, MovieLens, and SQuAD v1.1. Prompt engineering is frequently studied alongside disambiguation and mathematical reasoning, and some work reports that specific techniques can influence model generalization, interpretability, and flexibility.
